enumeration_literals.name: from_header
enumeration_literals.description:
This policy is for preserving an entry from another Zip
file. The extra field could be a Zip_64 or another kind.

array_types.name: Values_64
array_types.code: type Values_64 is array (1 .. 3) of Unsigned_64;

record_types.name: Central_File_Header

fields.name: internal_attributes
fields.description: internal properties of data

fields.name: external_attributes
fields.description: 1st byte if MS-DOS: see below

record_types.name: Data_Descriptor

fields.name: compressed_size
fields.description:
^ Stricto sensu, the data descriptor is 32 bit, but the actual
  size may be larger (complemented through a Zip64 extra field).

fields.name: uncompressed_size
fields.description:
^ Stricto sensu, the data descriptor is 32 bit, but the actual
  size may be larger (complemented through a Zip64 extra field).

record_types.name: End_of_Central_Dir

fields.name: disknum
fields.description:
The Zip archive may be appended to another file (for instance an
executable for self-extracting purposes) of size N.
Then, all offsets need to be shifted by N.
N=0 if the Zip archive is on its own.
The real offset of the end-of-central-dir
will be N + central_dir_size + central_dir_offset.
This way, we have an unique chance to determine N when reading the
end-of-central-dir. N is stored in the field hereafter:

fields.description:
The Zip archive may be appended to another file (for instance an

N=0 if the Zip archive is on its own.

will be N + central_dir_size + central_dir_offset.
This way, we have an unique chance to determine N when reading the
end-of-central-dir. N is stored in the field hereafter:

record_types.name: Zip64_End_of_Central_Dir_Locator

documentation.description: Bit 1:  If set, indicates an EOS marker is used.

Bit 11: Language encoding flag (EFS). If this bit is set, the filename and
        comment fields for this file MUST be encoded using UTF-8.
